metadata.description: Deep Suite — structured development, wiki, autonomous experimentation, independent code review, document gardening, and harness diagnostics plugins for Claude Code and Codex

plugins.name: deep-work
plugins.description: Evidence-Driven Development Protocol — all 24 command-equivalent entry surfaces promoted to `user-invocable: true` skills under skills/deep-{verb}/SKILL.md (Category A: 7 thin Skill() wrappers absorbed by their matching skill bodies; Category B: 17 new entry skills with frontmatter + Invocation/Inputs/Prerequisites head sections + byte-preserved bodies) for Claude Code skills / Codex / Copilot CLI / Gemini CLI / Agent SDK parity. **Fourth and final installment** of the suite-wide command→skill migration (after deep-docs v1.3.0 + deep-evolve v3.4.0 + deep-wiki v1.6.0 — closes cross-platform parity across the entire suite). Emits envelope-wrapped session-receipt + slice-receipt + handoff + compaction-state; non-implement dangerous-command denylist + phase-guard hook golden test + deep-status hub-spoke pattern (4 sub-skills dispatched via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/deep-X/SKILL.md inline Read) + plugin-validator 0/0/0 + skill-reviewer × 24 PASS (v6.7.1+) v6.7.1 adds native `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` metadata, Codex-facing `AGENTS.md`, Codex compatibility docs, and the skill-native `$deep-work:deep-work` primary entry alias while preserving Claude Code metadata. **v6.8.0** ships three coordinated themes — (1) plan-quality contract enforcement: every non-inline S/M/L slice now requires `failing_test`, `verification_cmd`, `expected_output`, `code_sketch`, `steps`, and the plan review-gate (`skills/shared/references/review-gate.md`) is aligned with that contract (v5.8 fallback removed); (2) CI hardening: advisory shellcheck step on `hooks/scripts/**/*.sh` (`continue-on-error: true`), test discovery widened to 6 subtree globs run sequentially via `--test-concurrency=1`, CI Node bumped 20 → 22 (LTS); (3) receipt-tracker robustness: pre-lock receipt init restored with `O_CREAT | O_EXCL` (`fs.writeFileSync` `flag: 'wx'`) so single-write slices keep canonical `SLICE-NNN.json` even on stale-lock timeout. Three new contract test files (`plan-quality-contract`, `ci-workflow-contract`, `file-tracker-lock-timeout`) pin all surfaces against silent drift. `npm test` 901/901 pass; three iterative 3-way deep-review rounds (Opus + Codex review + Codex adversarial) converged with APPROVE. **v6.9.0** wires deep-work as a read-only consumer of the new `deep-memory` v0.1.0 plugin via two opt-in affordances — (1) Phase 1 Research recall: `skills/deep-research/SKILL.md` cites `.deep-memory/latest-brief.md` verbatim under a new `## Cross-project Memory` section in `research.md` when the brief exists; absent → research artifact stays deep-memory-agnostic (privacy invariant — never auto-invoke, never write to artifact); provenance tokens (`mem-<ULID>` Crockford-base32 uppercase) extracted into `cross_project_memory.cited_memory_ids[]` state field for the Phase 4+ feedback hook; (2) Phase 5 Integrate harvest: `skills/deep-integrate/SKILL.md` proposes `/deep-memory-harvest` in the top-3 LLM recommendations + B-fallback list when `deep-memory ∈ plugins.installed` and the session changed > 0 files. `detect-plugins.sh` extended (TARGETS now 6 plugins) so the gate's `plugins.installed`/`plugins.missing` signal correctly enumerates deep-memory. `tests/deep-memory-integration.test.js` (12 fixture-based contract tests) + `detect-plugins.test.js` regression test pin every documented invariant. 3-round internal deep-review-loop converged to APPROVE. `npm test` 850/850 pass.

plugins.name: deep-wiki
plugins.description: v1.7.0 — large-wiki reader race fix + index.md dashboard (5-section, ~3-5KB stable token budget, explicit marker) + inbox stale cleanup (quarantine + partial_fail-protected) + npm test bare-discovery + wiki-setup dashboard seed

plugins.name: deep-evolve
plugins.description: Autonomous Experimentation Protocol — `/deep-evolve` promoted from a slash command to a `user-invocable: true` skill for Codex / Copilot CLI / Gemini CLI / Agent SDK parity (Step 0 / Step 0.5 / Step 1 parsing + Protocol Routing Summary unchanged); v3.4.1 trims `deep-evolve-workflow` description from 1137 → 905 chars to fit Codex's 1024-char skill description limit (Codex was silently dropping the workflow skill at load time). Emits envelope-wrapped evolve-receipt + evolve-insights + reverse handoff + compaction-state with identity-gated chain extraction + protect-readonly hook golden + 4-way manifest drift CI guard (v3.4.2+) v3.4.2 adds native `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` metadata, Codex-facing `AGENTS.md`, and Codex compatibility docs while preserving Claude Code metadata.

plugins.name: deep-review
plugins.description: Independent Evaluator — v1.7.1 adds agy fingerprint **hybrid mode** as the new default (~100× faster than v1.7.0 full-walk on large repos): `git status -z` + per-dirty-file SHA-256 + HEAD-sha + sensitive-pattern walk via shared `lib/sensitive-patterns.list` (52 patterns, read by both Python `scan_sensitive_files` and Bash `run-agy-reviewer.sh`). 3-mode dispatcher (`hybrid` | `full-walk` | `git-status` | `off`) + `AGY_FINGERPRINT_MODE` env var override + `agy_fingerprint_mode` config field with auto-migration from v1.7.0. Path-safe via NUL-separated git porcelain + hex-encoded path bytes; pipefail-shielded sensitive scan with PIPESTATUS-equivalent guard; symlink-safe `_resolve_symlink` library resolution; startup probes for sha256 backend + library presence with stderr-tail-visible degrade. 15-case §7 test matrix + 5 new pattern-family assertions + cross-consumer parity test. Validated through 5 spec / 4 plan / 4 impl rounds of `/deep-review-loop` convergence. CHANGELOG documents the "Behavior change (default)" with 1-line opt-out (`agy_fingerprint_mode: full-walk`) and 5 v1.7.2 follow-ups. Prior v1.7.0 4-way review (Opus + Codex review + Codex adversarial + agy) preserved.

plugins.name: deep-docs
plugins.description: Document gardening agent — emits envelope-wrapped last-scan.json + /deep-docs slash command → user-invocable skill conversion for cross-platform parity (Claude Code slash + Codex / Copilot CLI / Gemini CLI `Skill()` invocation) + verify-fixes.sh retargeted to skills/deep-docs/SKILL.md (43 grep checks) + plugin-dev validator round-2 PASS (v1.3.1+) v1.3.1 adds native `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` metadata, Codex-facing `AGENTS.md`, and Codex compatibility docs while preserving Claude Code metadata.

plugins.name: deep-dashboard
plugins.description: Cross-plugin harness diagnostics + suite telemetry — emits envelope-wrapped harnessability-report.json + envelope-aware collector + 16-metric aggregator (all M4-core post-M5.5: 3 M5-activated handoff/compaction + 1 M5.5-activated per-plugin test coverage from manifest mirror) + JSONL/markdown/OTLP exporters + catalog-drift CI guard with horizon mechanism (suite §9+ extensions reported as info, not drift) + cross-shell `npm test` glob portability (v1.3.7+) v1.3.7 moves Codex skill surfaces to `skills/<skill>/SKILL.md` directory layout so Codex can discover `deep-harnessability` and `deep-harness-dashboard` from the plugin manifest.

plugins.name: deep-memory
plugins.description: Cross-project semantic operational memory — harvests sibling deep-suite artifacts (recurring-findings, evolve-insights, session-receipts, docs-scan, wiki-index), distills them through a Hybrid pipeline (rule-based Step A → 5 mapper kinds + LLM sub-agent Step B with graceful candidate fallback) and persists M3-envelope-wrapped memory cards under ~/.deep-memory/. `/deep-memory-brief <task>` surfaces top-N memory briefs via a 6-stage ranking pipeline (FTS5 BM25 → privacy-scope SQL filter → hard filter → Unicode-aware tokenize → project_sim Jaccard → evidence quality sigmoid → applicability guard → diversity). 4 user-invocable skills (init / harvest / brief / audit) + 1 reference (memory-schema). 3-pass redaction symmetric across cards/events/quarantine. Project-lease + global-lock + idempotent event_key. better-sqlite3 in optionalDependencies (v0.1.3) — graceful degradation when native binding unavailable (cards/events still write, FTS5 upsert skipped + redacted warning surfaces). Atomic write + readback validate. v0.1.0 ships single-project-safe (multi-project memory_id collision documented for v0.2.0 redesign). v0.1.2 realigned 4 of 5 Step A mappers to sibling-real payload shapes (silent 0-card harvest blocker). v0.1.3 round-1 deep-review-loop hardening: better-sqlite3 → optionalDependencies, FTS load-error path redacted, cards.warnings regression coverage, env-gated DEEP_MEMORY_FULL_SUITE smoke. 252 tests PASS / 0 fail.
